title: AI Guardian Configuration
description: Configuration file for ai-guardian security tool that protects IDE AI agents from unauthorized tool access and security risks

permissions.description: Permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed.

oneOf.description: Object format with allow/deny arrays (backward compatible)

deny.description: Directories to scan for deny patterns

allow.description: Directories to scan for allow patterns

oneOf.description: Array format with mode in each entry (NEW: mode field actually used)

permissions_directories.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover permissions from directories. Most users should use remote_configs instead. Supports both object format (allow/deny arrays) and direct array format.

remote_configs.description: RECOMMENDED: Load policies from remote URLs (enterprise/team policies). This is the preferred way to manage permissions.

urls.description: List of remote configuration URLs to fetch

refresh_interval_hours.description: Hours between automatic refresh of remote configs

expire_after_hours.description: Hours until cached remote config expires

permissions_enabled.description: Global tool permissions enforcement control (NEW in v1.4.0). Object with 'enabled' property that supports boolean (permanent) or time-based (temporary) formats.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

secret_scanning.description: Secret scanning with Gitleaks control (NEW in v1.4.0). Object with 'enabled' property that supports boolean (permanent) or time-based (temporary) formats.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for test fixtures, examples, and documentation with fake credentials. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Examples: '**/tests/fixtures/**', '**/examples/**/*.example.*', '**/.gitleaks.toml'

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for tools that read test data or documentation. Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: [] (scan all tools), specific use cases vary. Most users should use ignore_files instead for test fixtures.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Enhanced secret detection patterns from a pattern server. Most users should use default Gitleaks patterns. Supports time-based disabling (NEW in v1.4.0).

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

prompt_injection.description: Prompt injection detection configuration (NEW in v1.2.0, time-based support in v1.4.0). Protects against prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

detector.description: Detection method: heuristic (local patterns, <1ms), rebuff/llm-guard (ML-based, requires setup)

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity: low (obvious attacks only), medium (balanced), high (more aggressive)

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns to ignore (for false positives). Supports time-based patterns.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Example: '**/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md' to ignore all skill documentation files.

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: 'Skill:code-review' (ignore specific skill), 'Skill:*' or 'Skill' (ignore all skills), 'mcp__*' (ignore all MCP tools). Useful for tools that legitimately read documentation containing example attack patterns.

directory_exclusions.description: Directory exclusions for .ai-read-deny blocking (NEW in v1.5.0). CRITICAL: .ai-read-deny markers ALWAYS take precedence over exclusions.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

paths.description: Directory paths to exclude from .ai-read-deny blocking. Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level. .ai-read-deny markers ALWAYS block (no config can override).

permission_rule.description: Permission rule defining allow/deny patterns for a specific tool matcher

mode.description: Permission mode: 'allow' creates an allow-list, 'deny' creates a deny-list

patterns.description: List of patterns to allow/deny. Supports simple strings or time-based objects with valid_until.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot add or modify rules for this matcher. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

time_based_pattern.description: Pattern with optional time-based expiration (NEW in v1.3.0)

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T12:00:00Z'). Pattern expires after this time.

time_based_feature.description: Feature toggle with optional time-based temporary disabling (NEW in v1.4.0)

disabled_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T18:00:00Z'). Feature override expires after this time.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the temporary change

directory_entry.description: Directory to scan for auto-discovery of permissions. In object format (allow/deny arrays), mode is determined by which array the entry is in. In array format, mode defaults to 'allow' if not specified.

matcher.description: Tool type to apply discovered patterns to (e.g., 'Skill', 'mcp__*'). Defaults to 'Skill' if not specified.

mode.description: Permission mode for discovered patterns. Only used in array format; ignored in object format (allow/deny arrays). Defaults to 'allow'.

url.description: Local directory path or GitHub URL to scan

token_env.description: Environment variable containing GitHub token (for private repos)

remote_url_entry.description: Remote configuration URL with optional settings

url.description: Remote configuration URL (https://) or local file path

enabled.description: Whether this remote config is enabled

token_env.description: Environment variable containing auth token for remote URL
